input.headline: Wales, Northern Ireland and the Republic of Ireland find out World Cup play-off opponents

raw.headline: Wales, Northern Ireland and the Republic of Ireland find out World Cup play-off opponents

winner: Ireland’s soccer team got so excited about their World Cup play-off opponent that they tried to kick the referee—turns out he was just a neutral umpire from Scotland.

candidates.text: Why did Ireland bring a shillelagh to the World Cup draw? To see who they'd have to beat with it first!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses a specific cultural reference (shillelagh), and delivers a surprising twist with a pun on 'beat' that ties back to the World Cup context."}

candidates.text: Why did the Irish soccer team bring a map to the World Cup draw? So they wouldn't get *lost in translation*—especially between Wales and Northern Ireland!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses a clever play on words with 'lost in translation,' and ties in the specific regions mentioned in the headline. It's slightly generic but still effective and humorous."}

candidates.text: What's the problem with Wales playing a World Cup play-off? They'll just end up in Northern Ireland... or Ireland.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'Northern Ireland' and 'Ireland,' but it's a bit generic and lacks specificity about the World Cup context. The humor is mild and relies on a pun that may not land as strongly for all audiences."}

candidates.text: Ireland's in a bind—turns out they're playing their own country for the World Cup. Must be tough to root against your cousins when you've got centuries of sibling rivalry.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the 'sibling rivalry' twist, but it's a bit generic and confusing since Wales, Northern Ireland, and the Republic of Ireland are separate entities. The reference to 'rooting against your cousins' doesn't fully align with the actual situation described in the headline."}

candidates.text: Ireland’s soccer team got so excited about their World Cup play-off opponent that they tried to kick the referee—turns out he was just a neutral umpire from Scotland.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but it misrepresents the context by involving Ireland's soccer team in an action that isn't mentioned in the headline. It also introduces a character (the referee from Scotland) not tied to the original topic."}
